items.label: Next.js 15
items.strength: SSR+SSG+PPR, App Router, edge-ready, React Server Components
items.weakness: Vercel-optimized, config complexity
items.tip: Use next/cache with revalidate for ISR. Avoid getServerSideProps in App Router.

items.label: SvelteKit 2
items.strength: Tiny bundle, Svelte 5 runes, zero-runtime reactivity
items.weakness: Smaller UI library ecosystem
items.tip: Use adapter-cloudflare for edge deploy. Svelte 5 runes replace Svelte stores.

items.label: Astro 5
items.strength: Zero JS default, islands architecture, content collections
items.weakness: Not for heavy interactivity
items.tip: Use content:collections for blog/docs. Islands for interactive components only.

items.label: React + Vite 6
items.strength: Fastest HMR, SPA, flexible, massive ecosystem
items.weakness: No SSR without Vite SSR plugin or wrapper
items.tip: Use React Query for data fetching. Zustand for state. TanStack Router for routing.

items.label: Nuxt 4
items.strength: Vue 3 + SSR/SSG/SPA in one, auto-imports, Nitro server engine
items.weakness: Vue ecosystem smaller than React
items.tip: Nitro runs on any runtime including Cloudflare Workers. Use useFetch for SSR-safe fetching.

items.label: Remix / React Router 7
items.strength: Nested routes, loader/action pattern, progressive enhancement
items.weakness: Different mental model from Next.js
items.tip: Loaders run on server per-route. Actions handle mutations. No useState for server data.

items.label: Supabase
items.strength: Postgres + Auth + Storage + Realtime + Edge Functions
items.weakness: Free tier pauses after 7 days inactivity
items.tip: Set up a cron ping to prevent sleep. Use RLS on every table — never bypass.

items.label: Turso (libSQL)
items.strength: SQLite at edge, 500 free DBs, 1B row reads/mo, no cold start
items.weakness: SQLite not for complex multi-table joins
items.tip: Use Drizzle ORM. Create a DB per tenant for multi-tenant apps (500 free DBs is generous).

items.label: Neon Postgres
items.strength: Serverless Postgres, DB branching, never pauses, connection pooling
items.weakness: Connection pooling required for serverless
items.tip: Always use connection pooler URL for serverless functions. Direct URL only for migrations.

items.label: PocketBase 0.22
items.strength: Single Go binary: DB+Auth+Files+Admin UI+Hooks. No config.
items.weakness: Self-managed, not auto-scaling
items.tip: Extend with Go hooks or JS hooks (pb_hooks). Pairs perfectly with Fly.io free VMs.

items.label: Cloudflare D1
items.strength: SQLite at edge, 5M reads/day free, co-located with Workers
items.weakness: Write-heavy workloads hit limits quickly
items.tip: Use for read-heavy data. Combine with KV for high-read cache layer.

items.label: MongoDB Atlas
items.strength: Flexible document model, Atlas free 512MB, built-in search
items.weakness: 512MB limit hits fast, M0 cluster has no SLA
items.tip: Enable Atlas Search (free) for full-text. Use $lookup sparingly — prefer embedding.

items.label: Groq API
items.strength: Fastest inference globally (700+ tok/s), Llama3/Mixtral/Gemma, 14k tokens/min free
items.weakness: Rate limits on free tier, limited model selection
items.tip: Use for real-time streaming chat. groq-sdk npm package. Add retry with exponential backoff.

items.label: Ollama (Local)
items.strength: 100% free, private, 100+ models, runs offline, no rate limits
items.weakness: Needs GPU/RAM locally, not hostable without a server
items.tip: Use llama3.1:8b for most tasks. codellama for code. qwen2.5-coder for Python/JS coding.

items.label: Gemini 2.0 Flash
items.strength: 1M context window, multimodal (image+audio+video), 1500 req/day free
items.weakness: Google data terms, privacy concerns for sensitive data
items.tip: Use for long-document analysis, image understanding, and multimodal workflows.

items.label: Cerebras Inference
items.strength: Wafer-scale chip — 2000+ tokens/sec on Llama3.1-70B, generous free tier
items.weakness: Limited model selection, newer platform
items.tip: Fastest large-model inference available. Free tier sufficient for most prototypes.

items.label: OpenRouter
items.strength: 100+ models, free tier includes Llama/Mistral/Gemma, unified API key
items.weakness: Latency varies by provider, reliability depends on upstream
items.tip: Use ':free' suffix models for zero cost. Set fallback models in request for reliability.

items.label: Nvidia Nemotron
items.strength: Nemotron-4 340B, enterprise-grade, Nvidia API free credits on signup
items.weakness: Credits limited, not a long-term free solution
items.tip: Best for enterprise reasoning tasks. Nemotron-4 340B outperforms GPT-4 on many benchmarks.

items.label: OpenCode / Qwen
items.strength: Alibaba Qwen2.5-Coder-32B free on HuggingFace, top coding benchmark scores
items.weakness: HF inference can be slow under load, rate limited
items.tip: Use via HuggingFace Inference API (free). Qwen2.5-Coder-32B beats GPT-4 on HumanEval.

items.label: Together AI
items.strength: $25 free credits on signup, Llama/Mistral/Qwen, fast inference
items.weakness: Credits expire, no perpetual free tier
items.tip: Redeem $25 signup credits immediately. Use for fine-tuning experiments and batch jobs.

items.label: Cloudflare Workers AI
items.strength: 10k neurons/day free, runs in Workers at edge, no cold start
items.weakness: Limited model selection, smaller context windows
items.tip: Use for lightweight inference at edge. AI Gateway adds caching + rate limiting on top.

items.label: Stripe Billing
items.strength: Industry standard, subscriptions+one-time+credits, best webhook reliability
items.weakness: 2.9%+30c per txn, requires business entity in some regions
items.tip: Use Stripe Billing for subscriptions. Payment Links for quick one-time sales with zero code.

items.label: Lemon Squeezy
items.strength: Merchant of record (handles VAT/tax globally), digital products focus
items.weakness: 8-10% total fees, less flexible than Stripe
items.tip: Use if you want zero tax complexity. They handle all global VAT automatically.

items.label: Polar.sh
items.strength: GitHub-native, built for OSS devs, issue funding, 5% fee
items.weakness: Smaller audience, newer platform
items.tip: Best for OSS projects. GitHub Sponsors integration. Benefit-based subscriptions.

items.label: Gumroad
items.strength: Built-in marketplace discovery, easiest setup, digital product focus
items.weakness: 10% fee, limited customization, no subscriptions on free
items.tip: Use for one-time digital products. 10% fee is high but zero setup cost. Good for testing demand.

items.label: Whop
items.strength: Built-in community features, Discord integration, great for courses+communities
items.weakness: 3-3.5% fee, newer platform
items.tip: Best for community memberships. Built-in access control for Discord/Telegram.
